completed_tasks.name: Verify whether current OpenWrt build has the same limitation

remaining_tasks.name: Design and implement live-process SIGSTOP/SIGCONT pause/resume with tests
Build and perform Docker plus OpenWrt end-to-end verification

blockers.description: No OpenWrt test container currently exists on the Docker test host; only stale OpenWrt images remain.
blockers.workaround: After implementing the fix, rebuild the IPK and recreate isolated OpenWrt 23.05 and 24.10 test containers without touching the six persistent Docker test volumes.

decisions.decision: Implement true in-process pause/resume using SIGSTOP and SIGCONT instead of terminating and relaunching tdl.
decisions.rationale: tdl 0.20.3 --continue only skips fully completed items. It does not resume byte ranges within a partially downloaded single file and recreates the .tmp file with os.Create.

decisions.decision: Keep tdl --continue for recovering completed items in multi-item downloads, but do not describe it as single-file byte resume.
decisions.rationale: This matches upstream tdl semantics and avoids discarding useful task-level recovery.

decisions.decision: Apply the same pause/resume implementation to Docker and OpenWrt through the shared tg_downloader_ui/app.py.
decisions.rationale: The OpenWrt IPK packages the same application file and uses the same tdl 0.20.3 behavior.

next_action: Read .planning/.continue-here.md, acknowledge its blocking constraints, then write failing process-level tests for pause retaining the same PID and resume continuing the same .tmp file before changing app.py.
context_notes: No pause/resume code changes were made. Git was clean at d3b53fe before creating the handoff. The previous 9eee2c0 fix was a false completion: tests asserted --continue was present but never verified byte reuse with a real partial file.
